title: Helpdesk Compliant Example
transcript: Agent: Thanks for calling the helpdesk. Before I reset or unlock your account, I need to verify your identity first. Once we've confirmed your details, I'll issue a temporary password right away.

title: Travel Partial Disclosure
transcript: Agent: I can move you onto the earlier flight. There will be a seventy-five dollar change fee for the exchange, and if you're happy with that I can confirm the new itinerary now.

title: Helpdesk Non-Compliant Example
transcript: Agent: I'll unlock the account for you now so you can get back in, and if the system asks me for anything else we'll verify your details after that.

title: Helpdesk Conversation Passes 101 Fails 102
transcript:
Agent: Thanks for calling the helpdesk, this is Maya speaking. How can I help today?
User: Hi Maya, I got locked out after too many password attempts and I need to get back in before a client meeting.
Agent: I can help with that. Before I reset or unlock your account, I need to verify your identity first.
User: Sure, no problem.
Agent: Please confirm your employee ID for me.
User: It's SG48291.
Agent: Thanks. I've also sent a one-time code to your registered mobile number ending in 4421. Please read that code back when you receive it.
User: I have it now, the code is 318604.
Agent: Great, that matches what I see here, so your identity is verified.
User: Perfect, can you unlock it now?
Agent: Yes, I'm unlocking the account now and I'll also issue a temporary password so you can sign in immediately.
User: Thank you.
Agent: Your temporary password has been sent to your work email, and you'll be prompted to create a new one after login.
User: That's fine.
Agent: Please try signing in again in the next minute, and if anything still looks blocked, stay on the line and I'll check it with you.
User: I just got the email, so I'll try it now. Thanks for the help.

title: Travel Conversation Fails 101 Passes 102
transcript:
Agent: Good afternoon, you've reached the travel desk, this is Daniel. How can I help today?
Customer: Hi Daniel, I need to move my flight from Friday evening to the Saturday morning departure.
Agent: I can check that for you now.
Customer: Great, I just need the earliest option available.
Agent: I have a seat on the Saturday 8:10 a.m. flight.
Customer: That sounds fine.
Agent: Before I confirm this booking change, there is a change fee that will apply.
Customer: Okay, how much is that fee?
Agent: The airline change fee is eighty dollars for this exchange.
Customer: All right.
Agent: There is also a fare difference on the new itinerary, and this new flight is fifty dollars higher than your current ticket, so you would need to pay that extra amount as well.
Customer: So the total additional cost is one hundred thirty dollars?
Agent: That's right, eighty dollars for the change fee and fifty dollars for the higher fare on the new itinerary.
Customer: Okay, please go ahead.
Agent: Thanks, I'm confirming the change now and I'll send the updated itinerary to your email in the next few minutes.
Customer: Perfect, thank you for sorting that out.

title: Travel Conversation With Borderline Phrase
transcript:
Agent: Good morning, this is Amelia at the travel desk. How can I help?
Customer: Hi Amelia, I want to move my return flight from Sunday night to Monday morning.
Agent: I can check what is available.
Customer: Thank you, I just need the earliest Monday option.
Agent: I have one leaving at 8:20 a.m. with the same airline.
Customer: That sounds promising.
Agent: There will be a fee to make the change, and the new flight is a bit higher.
Customer: Okay, can you explain that a little more?
Agent: The airline is treating it as a standard voluntary change.
Customer: Understood.
Agent: I am still waiting for the fare desk to finish updating the exchange screen.
Customer: No worries.
Agent: Once that refreshes, I can tell you whether you want to proceed.
Customer: Fine.
Agent: I just want to make sure the routing still works for you before I touch the booking.
Customer: Yes, Monday morning is still best.
Agent: All right, I am keeping the option on hold for the next few minutes.
Customer: Thanks.
Agent: If you decide to move ahead, I can finalize it once the pricing view settles.
Customer: Okay, please let me know when you have it.

title: Helpdesk Adversarial: Verify After Unlock
transcript:
Agent: Thanks for contacting support, this is Omar. What is happening today?
User: My account is locked after too many attempts.
Agent: I can help you get back in.
User: Please unlock it as soon as possible.
Agent: I will unlock your account first so you can continue working.
User: Thank you.
Agent: The unlock request is going through now. After I unlock your account, I will verify your identity for our records. The account

title: Combined Conversation Passes 101 and 102
transcript:
Agent: Welcome to the service desk, this is Priya speaking. How can I help today?
Customer: Hi Priya, I am locked out of my employee portal and I also need to change a flight booked through the company travel desk.
Agent: I can help with both requests.
Customer: Great, the account issue is the urgent one.
Agent: Before I reset or unlock your account, I need to verify your identity first.
Customer: Sure.
Agent: Please confirm your employee ID and the one-time code I just sent to your registered mobile.
Customer: My employee ID is SG57124 and the code is 441902.
Agent: Thank you, that matches our records, so your identity is verified.
Customer: Perfect.
Agent: I have now unlocked your account and sent a temporary password to your work email.
Customer: Thanks. Can we handle the flight change now?
Agent: Yes. I found a seat on the later flight tomorrow evening.
Customer: That option works for me.
Agent: Before I confirm this booking change, there is a change fee that will apply.
Customer: Okay, how much is the fee?
Agent: The airline change fee is sixty dollars for this exchange.
Customer: Understood.
Agent: There is also a fare difference on the new itinerary, so you will either pay the extra amount or receive the balance as travel credit.
Customer: What does that look like for this flight?
Agent: In this case the new itinerary is forty dollars higher, so the total additional amount is one hundred dollars before I confirm the change.
Customer: That is fine, please go ahead.
Agent: Thanks, I am confirming the booking change now and I will email the updated itinerary right away.
Customer: Excellent, thank you for your help.
